romania.title: TollRo, amânat pentru a doua oară. Când va intra în vigoare noul sistem de taxare a drumurilor
romania.summary: Aplicarea noului sistem electronic de taxare a drumurilor naționale din România, TollRo, a fost amânată pentru a doua oară. Camera Deputaților a adoptat miercuri proiectul de lege pentru aprobarea OUG nr. 74/2025, care prevede prorogarea termenului de aplicare a sistemului până la 1 octombrie 2026. Proiectul a fost adoptat cu 244 de voturi „pentru”.

Potrivit martorilor, acesta l-ar fi luat în brațe pe copil și ar fi ieșit în grabă din clasă, provocând panică în rândul profesorilor.

medical.title: The science of sweat: A researcher is helping Brazil prepare for the heat of the World Cup
medical.summary: As the world's best soccer players prepare for the 2026 FIFA World Cup across North America this summer, teams are gearing up not only for opponents but also for the heat. In stadiums from Miami to Mexico City, soaring temperatures and humidity could affect matches, recovery times and ultimately championship outcomes. And behind one of the most storied teams in soccer history is a University of Florida scientist using the science of sweat to help athletes perform, recover and stay healthy in extreme heat.

medical.title: 1 in 3 middle-aged adults struggle with basic 'everyday' health tasks, study finds
medical.summary: A new Northwestern University study has found one in three middle-aged American adults ages 35 to 64 cannot consistently read prescription instructions correctly, understand medical forms or recall details from doctor visits involving chronic condition diagnoses. These skills—often referred to as health literacy—are critical for managing common conditions such as high blood pressure, high cholesterol and diabetes, which commonly emerge in midlife.

medical.title: Japan's quality of life kept sliding after COVID restrictions ended, study reveals
medical.summary: A nationwide study tracking Japanese adults before, during and after the COVID-19 pandemic found that health-related quality of life steadily declined over seven years and did not rebound after the public health emergency ended. Researchers say the decline may reflect the cumulative impact of pandemic-related changes in physical activity, mental well-being and social interaction among working-age adults across Japan.

medical.title: Inexpensive drug should be used in most major surgeries to prevent blood transfusion, clinical trial finds
medical.summary: A landmark clinical trial published in the New England Journal of Medicine found that tranexamic acid reduced transfusions across major surgeries without increasing the risk of dangerous blood clots. The trial was coordinated by the University of Manitoba (UM) and co-led by researchers at UM and The Ottawa Hospital.

medical.title: New study flips mental health paradigm: Proactive brain training builds community resilience before crisis hits
medical.summary: A new study published in Frontiers in Psychology challenges the traditional, reactive model of mental health care by demonstrating that proactive brain training can strengthen the human mind before mental health challenges take root. Additionally, it can support the wellness of those with a history of mental illness.

medical.title: Canada faces surge in social anxiety, new research shows
medical.summary: A new Canadian study has found that social anxiety disorder (SAD) now affects nearly one in seven adults—a 71% increase since 2002—making it one of the most common mental health challenges in the country. The study is published in the journal Psychiatry Research.

medical.title: A classic brain test exposed AI's biggest weakness
medical.summary: Researchers gave top AI models a classic attention test used in psychology and found a major flaw. While the models could correctly name colors in short lists, their performance deteriorated sharply as the task became longer and more complex. Some leading systems fell from over 90% accuracy to nearly complete failure.

medical.title: Scientists mapped every neural connection in a fruit fly and found a surprise
medical.summary: A groundbreaking new connectome maps every neural connection in an adult fruit fly’s central nervous system, creating an unprecedented view of how the brain and body work together. The findings suggest that complex behaviors emerge from distributed local circuits rather than a single central controller, offering new clues about intelligence, movement, and brain function.

medical.title: Intermittent fasting triggers surprising changes in the brain
medical.summary: Losing weight may involve rewiring the gut and the brain at the same time. In a study of obese adults, an intermittent fasting-style diet led to significant weight loss, healthier metabolic markers, and notable shifts in gut bacteria. Brain scans also revealed changes in regions tied to appetite, cravings, and self-control. The results suggest the gut microbiome and brain may work together to influence weight-loss success.

science.title: NASA Webb Finds Strongest Evidence Yet for ‘Black Hole Stars’
science.summary: The complex puzzle known as little red dots has become more complete since their initial discovery by NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope in 2022. Now a particular little red dot’s spectrum is helping connect many of the pieces. A team of astronomers led by Vasily Kokorev at the University of Texas at Austin identified the […]

science.title: Chimpanzees react negatively to unfairness, especially when close partners are nearby
science.summary: Primates, including humans, are among the social animals living on Earth. Their survival relies heavily on cooperation with others, alliances, the sharing of resources and other social interactions.

science.title: Economist finds financial inequity distorted centuries-old Spanish water inequality
science.summary: For generations, economists have viewed markets as the gold standard of efficiency, assuming resources naturally flow to the people who value them most. But new research from the University of Miami Patti and Allan Herbert Business School suggests that assumption can break down when participants cannot afford to compete on equal footing.

science.title: Astronomers find a four-carbon sugar in deep space
science.summary: The space between stars may seem like a barren desert, but over the past few decades scientists have been finding all sorts of interesting chemicals in it. From the precursors to proteins to the building blocks of cell membranes, there has been discovery after discovery of new molecules in the giant gas clouds between the stars. Now, a new paper available on the arXiv preprint server details the discovery of the first-ever four-carbon sugar in the interstellar medium (ISM), and it is another brick on the path to understanding how life on Earth first developed.

science.title: Parents helping kids enjoy math may boost achievement as much as content support
science.summary: How do children learn math? It's shaped by what they know as well as their motivation and engagement. Historically, research on children's math learning has been focused on parents' cognitive practices (such as math talk—informal conversations that involve math), however emerging evidence shows how parents' motivational practices (encouraging independence and helping children enjoy math) may also play a critical role in their math abilities.

science.title: GLOBE Mission Earth Educators Participate in Land Cover Community of Practice
science.summary: During the 2025-2026 school year, educators from the NASA Science Activation Program’s GLOBE (Global Learning and Observation to Benefit the Environment) Mission Earth project participated in a specialized Community of Practice led by NASA Langley Research Center to refine how students interact with NASA’s land cover data (MODIS, Landsat, and Sentinel-2).

science.title: NASA’s CloudCube Pioneers Miniaturized Radar to Study Clouds, Precipitation
science.summary: A compact, multifrequency radar built by a team at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory will make it easier to collect information about dynamic cloud systems. Called CloudCube, this new instrument simultaneously probes the atmosphere with three radar signals, spanning 36 to 240 GHz, for optimized sensitivity to a wide range of water droplet and ice particle sizes.

science.title: Majorana modes withstand disorder in atomic chains, boosting fault-tolerant quantum computing
science.summary: Quantum computers—systems that process information and perform computations by leveraging the principles of quantum mechanics—could solve some tasks faster and more effectively than classical computers. While some studies have demonstrated the advantages of these computers for specific tasks, ensuring their reliable operation in real-world settings has proved challenging.

science.title: How cultural backgrounds shape financial forecasts
science.summary: There's a funny thing about facts. Two people can look at the same ones but interpret them very differently, depending on their styles of thinking. On Wall Street, analysts with short-term or long-term orientations may issue different financial forecasts for the same company, says Yong Yu, professor of accounting at the McCombs School of Business at The University of Texas at Austin.

science.title: Italian astronaut expects home flavors on Artemis III menu
science.summary: Italian astronaut Luca Parmitano is hoping to bring a taste of his homeland to the Artemis III mission he will pilot near Earth in 2027 to test two lunar modules.

environment.title: Experts say ‘bare bones’ US laws are unfit to regulate nascent deep-sea mining industry
environment.summary: This is part 2 of a two-part series examining the U.S.’s efforts to begin deep-sea mining in federal waters. Part 2 examines the regulations that would govern the industry. Part 1 explored the process behind proposed lease sales in U.S. federal waters and reactions to those plans. The deep-sea mining industry could launch in the […]

environment.title: Colombia passes landmark cattle traceability law to combat illegal deforestation
environment.summary: Colombia passed a landmark law June 4 aimed at improving traceability of its cattle supply chain to ensure beef isn’t sourced from deforested land. The law hopes to enhance existing traceability systems and make it easier to identify when cattle have grazed in protected areas and forests that were illegally cleared for pasture. “This is […]

environment.title: Kenya’s former Chief Justice David Maraga arrested at protest of national park construction
environment.summary: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s former Chief Justice David Maraga said he was arrested Monday alongside other activists protesting planned construction inside Nairobi National Park. Police fired tear gas canisters at the protesters who were marching outside the park while carrying banners with messages denouncing land grabs. Maraga was detained and later released while staging […]

environment.title: Urban wildlife is changing from the inside out (commentary)
environment.summary: Cities are expanding faster than at any point in human history, and wildlife is adapting in remarkable ways. We often talk about visible changes like animals becoming bolder, shifting their diets, or altering their daily rhythms to avoid people. But there is a deeper transformation happening inside their bodies, one that conservation science has barely […]

environment.title: Scientists discover vast hidden structure beneath Antarctica’s ice
environment.summary: A giant fan-shaped network of hidden basins has been discovered beneath East Antarctica, revealing that several well-known subglacial features are actually part of one massive geological structure. The finding sheds new light on Antarctica’s ancient tectonic history and could help scientists better understand how the ice sheet behaves today.

environment.title: This prehistoric fish may explain how animals first walked on Earth
environment.summary: Scientists have peered inside the skull of a 380-million-year-old Antarctic fish that was closely related to the first animals to walk on land, revealing surprising clues about how life began its move out of the water. Using advanced neutron imaging, researchers discovered that Koharalepis jarviki had features suited for living near the water’s surface, including openings in its skull that may have helped it gulp air and a light-sensitive organ linked to day-night rhythms.

environment.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
environment.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. The discovery is so unusual that researchers say these crab-like “chelae” evolved independently in this lineage, making it only the fourth known example of such structures appearing in insects at all.

environment.title: Scientists discover why some DNA-doubled cells refuse to die
environment.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising twist in how cells behave when division goes wrong. Sometimes a cell successfully copies its DNA but fails to split into two, leaving it with double the genetic material — a mistake linked to aging, cancer, and other major diseases. Researchers discovered that not all of these failures are equal.

mix_items.title: 😂 Din neatenție, Alfred Simonis s-a încălțat azi cu banii din cutia de pantofi, nu cu pantofii
mix_items.summary: Pentru că se consideră un politician respectabil, Alfred Simonis are acasă zeci de cutii de pantofi, dar iată că acest lucru uneori poate să îl și încurce. Azi, de exemplu, Simonis se grăbea să ajungă la ceva tăiere de panglică și, din neatenție, s-a încălțat cu teancurile de euro dintr-o cutie de pantofi, nu cu […] The post Din neatenție, Alfred Simonis s-a încălțat azi cu banii din cutia de pantofi, nu cu pantofii appeared first on Times New Roman .

mix_items.title: TollRo, amânat pentru a doua oară. Când va intra în vigoare noul sistem de taxare a drumurilor
mix_items.summary: Aplicarea noului sistem electronic de taxare a drumurilor naționale din România, TollRo, a fost amânată pentru a doua oară. Camera Deputaților a adoptat miercuri proiectul de lege pentru aprobarea OUG nr. 74/2025, care prevede prorogarea termenului de aplicare a sistemului până la 1 octombrie 2026. Proiectul a fost adoptat cu 244 de voturi „pentru”.

mix_items.summary: Au fost momente tensionate într-o școală din Sălaj: un bărbat de 59 de ani, cetățean italian, a intrat într-o sală de clasă pentru a-și vedea fiul în vârstă de 7 ani. Potrivit martorilor, acesta l-ar fi luat în brațe pe copil și ar fi ieșit în grabă din clasă, provocând panică în rândul profesorilor.

mix_items.title: The science of sweat: A researcher is helping Brazil prepare for the heat of the World Cup
mix_items.summary: As the world's best soccer players prepare for the 2026 FIFA World Cup across North America this summer, teams are gearing up not only for opponents but also for the heat. In stadiums from Miami to Mexico City, soaring temperatures and humidity could affect matches, recovery times and ultimately championship outcomes. And behind one of the most storied teams in soccer history is a University of Florida scientist using the science of sweat to help athletes perform, recover and stay healthy in extreme heat.

mix_items.title: 1 in 3 middle-aged adults struggle with basic 'everyday' health tasks, study finds
mix_items.summary: A new Northwestern University study has found one in three middle-aged American adults ages 35 to 64 cannot consistently read prescription instructions correctly, understand medical forms or recall details from doctor visits involving chronic condition diagnoses. These skills—often referred to as health literacy—are critical for managing common conditions such as high blood pressure, high cholesterol and diabetes, which commonly emerge in midlife.

mix_items.title: Chimpanzees react negatively to unfairness, especially when close partners are nearby
mix_items.summary: Primates, including humans, are among the social animals living on Earth. Their survival relies heavily on cooperation with others, alliances, the sharing of resources and other social interactions.

mix_items.title: Japan's quality of life kept sliding after COVID restrictions ended, study reveals
mix_items.summary: A nationwide study tracking Japanese adults before, during and after the COVID-19 pandemic found that health-related quality of life steadily declined over seven years and did not rebound after the public health emergency ended. Researchers say the decline may reflect the cumulative impact of pandemic-related changes in physical activity, mental well-being and social interaction among working-age adults across Japan.

mix_items.title: Economist finds financial inequity distorted centuries-old Spanish water inequality
mix_items.summary: For generations, economists have viewed markets as the gold standard of efficiency, assuming resources naturally flow to the people who value them most. But new research from the University of Miami Patti and Allan Herbert Business School suggests that assumption can break down when participants cannot afford to compete on equal footing.

mix_items.title: Astronomers find a four-carbon sugar in deep space
mix_items.summary: The space between stars may seem like a barren desert, but over the past few decades scientists have been finding all sorts of interesting chemicals in it. From the precursors to proteins to the building blocks of cell membranes, there has been discovery after discovery of new molecules in the giant gas clouds between the stars. Now, a new paper available on the arXiv preprint server details the discovery of the first-ever four-carbon sugar in the interstellar medium (ISM), and it is another brick on the path to understanding how life on Earth first developed.

mix_items.title: Parents helping kids enjoy math may boost achievement as much as content support
mix_items.summary: How do children learn math? It's shaped by what they know as well as their motivation and engagement. Historically, research on children's math learning has been focused on parents' cognitive practices (such as math talk—informal conversations that involve math), however emerging evidence shows how parents' motivational practices (encouraging independence and helping children enjoy math) may also play a critical role in their math abilities.

mix_items.title: Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge
mix_items.summary: Alexandru are 10 ani, locuiește într-un sat din județul Giurgiu și este elev în clasa a III‑a. Este un copil cu... Articolul Visul lui Alexandru începe cu o minge apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: GLOBE Mission Earth Educators Participate in Land Cover Community of Practice
mix_items.summary: During the 2025-2026 school year, educators from the NASA Science Activation Program’s GLOBE (Global Learning and Observation to Benefit the Environment) Mission Earth project participated in a specialized Community of Practice led by NASA Langley Research Center to refine how students interact with NASA’s land cover data (MODIS, Landsat, and Sentinel-2).

mix_items.title: Inexpensive drug should be used in most major surgeries to prevent blood transfusion, clinical trial finds
mix_items.summary: A landmark clinical trial published in the New England Journal of Medicine found that tranexamic acid reduced transfusions across major surgeries without increasing the risk of dangerous blood clots. The trial was coordinated by the University of Manitoba (UM) and co-led by researchers at UM and The Ottawa Hospital.

mix_items.title: New study flips mental health paradigm: Proactive brain training builds community resilience before crisis hits
mix_items.summary: A new study published in Frontiers in Psychology challenges the traditional, reactive model of mental health care by demonstrating that proactive brain training can strengthen the human mind before mental health challenges take root. Additionally, it can support the wellness of those with a history of mental illness.

mix_items.title: NASA’s CloudCube Pioneers Miniaturized Radar to Study Clouds, Precipitation
mix_items.summary: A compact, multifrequency radar built by a team at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory will make it easier to collect information about dynamic cloud systems. Called CloudCube, this new instrument simultaneously probes the atmosphere with three radar signals, spanning 36 to 240 GHz, for optimized sensitivity to a wide range of water droplet and ice particle sizes.

mix_items.title: Majorana modes withstand disorder in atomic chains, boosting fault-tolerant quantum computing
mix_items.summary: Quantum computers—systems that process information and perform computations by leveraging the principles of quantum mechanics—could solve some tasks faster and more effectively than classical computers. While some studies have demonstrated the advantages of these computers for specific tasks, ensuring their reliable operation in real-world settings has proved challenging.

mix_items.title: How cultural backgrounds shape financial forecasts
mix_items.summary: There's a funny thing about facts. Two people can look at the same ones but interpret them very differently, depending on their styles of thinking. On Wall Street, analysts with short-term or long-term orientations may issue different financial forecasts for the same company, says Yong Yu, professor of accounting at the McCombs School of Business at The University of Texas at Austin.

mix_items.title: Canada faces surge in social anxiety, new research shows
mix_items.summary: A new Canadian study has found that social anxiety disorder (SAD) now affects nearly one in seven adults—a 71% increase since 2002—making it one of the most common mental health challenges in the country. The study is published in the journal Psychiatry Research.

mix_items.title: A classic brain test exposed AI's biggest weakness
mix_items.summary: Researchers gave top AI models a classic attention test used in psychology and found a major flaw. While the models could correctly name colors in short lists, their performance deteriorated sharply as the task became longer and more complex. Some leading systems fell from over 90% accuracy to nearly complete failure.

mix_items.title: Scientists mapped every neural connection in a fruit fly and found a surprise
mix_items.summary: A groundbreaking new connectome maps every neural connection in an adult fruit fly’s central nervous system, creating an unprecedented view of how the brain and body work together. The findings suggest that complex behaviors emerge from distributed local circuits rather than a single central controller, offering new clues about intelligence, movement, and brain function.

mix_items.title: Italian astronaut expects home flavors on Artemis III menu
mix_items.summary: Italian astronaut Luca Parmitano is hoping to bring a taste of his homeland to the Artemis III mission he will pilot near Earth in 2027 to test two lunar modules.

mix_items.title: Experts say ‘bare bones’ US laws are unfit to regulate nascent deep-sea mining industry
mix_items.summary: This is part 2 of a two-part series examining the U.S.’s efforts to begin deep-sea mining in federal waters. Part 2 examines the regulations that would govern the industry. Part 1 explored the process behind proposed lease sales in U.S. federal waters and reactions to those plans. The deep-sea mining industry could launch in the […]

mix_items.title: Colombia passes landmark cattle traceability law to combat illegal deforestation
mix_items.summary: Colombia passed a landmark law June 4 aimed at improving traceability of its cattle supply chain to ensure beef isn’t sourced from deforested land. The law hopes to enhance existing traceability systems and make it easier to identify when cattle have grazed in protected areas and forests that were illegally cleared for pasture. “This is […]

mix_items.title: Kenya’s former Chief Justice David Maraga arrested at protest of national park construction
mix_items.summary: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s former Chief Justice David Maraga said he was arrested Monday alongside other activists protesting planned construction inside Nairobi National Park. Police fired tear gas canisters at the protesters who were marching outside the park while carrying banners with messages denouncing land grabs. Maraga was detained and later released while staging […]

mix_items.title: Urban wildlife is changing from the inside out (commentary)
mix_items.summary: Cities are expanding faster than at any point in human history, and wildlife is adapting in remarkable ways. We often talk about visible changes like animals becoming bolder, shifting their diets, or altering their daily rhythms to avoid people. But there is a deeper transformation happening inside their bodies, one that conservation science has barely […]

mix_items.title: Scientists discover vast hidden structure beneath Antarctica’s ice
mix_items.summary: A giant fan-shaped network of hidden basins has been discovered beneath East Antarctica, revealing that several well-known subglacial features are actually part of one massive geological structure. The finding sheds new light on Antarctica’s ancient tectonic history and could help scientists better understand how the ice sheet behaves today.

mix_items.title: Intermittent fasting triggers surprising changes in the brain
mix_items.summary: Losing weight may involve rewiring the gut and the brain at the same time. In a study of obese adults, an intermittent fasting-style diet led to significant weight loss, healthier metabolic markers, and notable shifts in gut bacteria. Brain scans also revealed changes in regions tied to appetite, cravings, and self-control. The results suggest the gut microbiome and brain may work together to influence weight-loss success.

mix_items.title: This prehistoric fish may explain how animals first walked on Earth
mix_items.summary: Scientists have peered inside the skull of a 380-million-year-old Antarctic fish that was closely related to the first animals to walk on land, revealing surprising clues about how life began its move out of the water. Using advanced neutron imaging, researchers discovered that Koharalepis jarviki had features suited for living near the water’s surface, including openings in its skull that may have helped it gulp air and a light-sensitive organ linked to day-night rhythms.

mix_items.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
mix_items.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. The discovery is so unusual that researchers say these crab-like “chelae” evolved independently in this lineage, making it only the fourth known example of such structures appearing in insects at all.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover why some DNA-doubled cells refuse to die
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising twist in how cells behave when division goes wrong. Sometimes a cell successfully copies its DNA but fails to split into two, leaving it with double the genetic material — a mistake linked to aging, cancer, and other major diseases. Researchers discovered that not all of these failures are equal.

joke_ro.text: Ce ai rezolvat? - Un sendvis si cateva emotii.

satire_ro.title: Răzbunarea băncilor! Au plătit amenda de 700.000.000 de euro cu fise de 1 ban
